sisyphus.prompt_append:
SPECIALIST AGENTS via pimp router — use ONLY when no oh-my-openagent built-in fits.

Built-in agents always take priority:
  explore, librarian, oracle, metis, momus, multimodal-looker, Sisyphus-Junior, plan

When you need a domain specialist not covered by the above, route through pimp:
  task(subagent_type="pimp", load_skills=[], run_in_background=false,
    description="<task>",
    prompt="Use specialist: <Name>. Task: <details>")

Common domain specialists (via pimp):
• "Backend Architect"         → system design, DB schema, APIs, microservices, AWS/K8s
• "DevOps Automator"          → Terraform, Helm, K8s, GitHub Actions, ArgoCD, EKS, CI/CD
• "Security Engineer"         → threat modeling, IAM, secrets, auth flows, vuln review
• "Code Reviewer"             → PR review, code quality, pattern consistency, tech debt
• "Technical Writer"          → docs, README, architecture docs, API reference, runbooks
• "Data Engineer"             → ETL, pipelines, Postgres/SQL optimisation, schema design
• "Infrastructure Maintainer" → reliability, monitoring, cost, performance analysis
• "Frontend Developer"        → React, TypeScript, component architecture, state management
• "Senior Developer"          → complex full-stack implementation
• "Performance Benchmarker"   → profiling, bottlenecks, benchmark design
• "Legal Compliance Checker"  → PCI DSS, GDPR, SOC2, ISO 27001
• "AI Engineer"               → Bedrock/ML integration, AI-powered features

Full catalog of all 69: skill(name="agent-directory")

ultrabrain.description: Use ONLY for genuinely hard, logic-heavy tasks. Give clear goals only, not step-by-step instructions.

deep.description: Goal-oriented autonomous problem-solving. Thorough research before action. For hairy problems requiring deep understanding.

artistry.description: Complex problem-solving with unconventional, creative approaches - beyond standard patterns

quick.description: Trivial tasks - single file changes, typo fixes, simple modifications
